entry.title: Chapter 1.2 - Substitute Marriage
entry.content:
Chapter 1.2: Substitute Marriage

This fantasy masterwork is known as «Capture the Demon», written by Fu Zhou, the queen of melodramatic romance novels, who made a pivotal switch into the genre by “returning after 10 years with this magnificent breakout novel”.

When she was young and naive, Ling Miaomiao had been deceived into shedding many tears for these melodramatic romance novels where characters were tragically fated to separate in life or death. Ten years later, due to sentimentality, she stayed up all night to reread Fu Zhou’s works. However, all she got in return were heavy sighs coming from her dormitory bed late at night.

What kind of transformative fantasy masterpiece is this? Under the hollow framework of a demon capture fantasy world, it was still the same cheesy romance story! The three female characters who pine for the male protagonist fight each other in a battle of wits and bravery, the male supporting character who’s infatuated with the heroine but cannot win her love, the hero and heroine trapped in layers of misunderstandings, a pair of lovebirds privately scheming in romance but also acting coquettishly sweet and buddy-buddy towards one another…the emotional developments were all extremely dramatic.

For this reason, Ling Miaomiao angrily picked up her pen to write. Before starting the book review, she heartfeltly selected a representative character – Ling Yu – as her entry criticism.

If arousing anger in the reader was a measure of success, then the third female character Ling Yu should be considered the most successfully portrayed character in the entire book.

She was terrible.

But she was not horrible by stereotypical standards. She would constantly exploit the kindness of others by playing the role of the hapless victim, requiting good favor with unbridled enmity. She backstabbed her caretakers while assuming the guise of a delicate, fragile lady.

This character remained mean-spirited and cowardly from start to end. She yearned for the hero but dared not compete head-on with the heroine for his affection. All she does, except for imagining how to obtain the hero in her perverted fantasies, is secretly sow discord and force the heroine into precarious plights.

If the villainous second female character had the imposing might of a proud and fierce tigress, then Ling Yu was a mouse that scuttled in the dark and gnawed on people’s toes. Or perhaps she’s a mere insect that burrows into rice barrels.

While leeching off the protection of the protagonists, she was also scheming how to disrupt the balance and undermine their relationship. She’s just like moss growing in a cold, moist, and dark place – difficult to shed and cast off.

Ling Miaomiao felt physically repulsed by this type of person. By comparison, the spoiled, headstrong, overtly evil second female character Princess Duan Yang was far more likeable.

Therefore, Ling Miaomiao indignantly picked up her pen to criticize Ling Yu, declaring her as “the most nauseating female supporting character of the year”. In the next second, Ling Miaomiao opened her eyes and unexpectedly found herself transported into the world of <<Capture the Demon>>. She had transmigrated into the most vile and disgusting character Ling Yu, and was required to complete special missions assigned by the system before she could return to her world.

What a horribly ironic situation.

entry.title: Chapter 1.1 - Substitute Marriage
entry.content:
Chapter 1.1: Substitute Marriage

The young girl in red clothes sat in front of the dressing table.

Her long flowing hair was neatly coiled up and adorned with a glittering phoenix head ornament. It drooped right above the girl’s smooth forehead, holding a pearl in its mouth.

A large red camellia hairpin was diagonally affixed to the side of her head. The camellia’s petals had started withering along its edges, as though the flower was hastily snatched from a vase arrangement in the afternoon, instead of plucked freshly from the garden.

For the garden was already devoid of flowers.

At nightfall, the dim sky appearing to be poured over with black ink, thunder rumbling like the roar of a wild beast, the heavy downpour came crashing like the cacophonous sound of ten thousand horses galloping. Needless to say, the defenseless flowers had been thoroughly thrashed into tiny red fragments by the rain.

She caressed the withered petals with her delicate bony fingers, thinking: Even if I’m in a hurry, this should be a time of celebration.

The person in the mirror smiled faintly. Today is my wedding day.

Her smile widened, but her pallid face abruptly stiffened and in an instant, shifted into a lifeless mask. In the next moment, those facial muscles twitched slightly again – her smiling expression slowly disappeared from view.

The person in the mirror’s eyes, originally overflowing with infatuation, now gleamed with curiosity and calmness.

Slouching sideways, Ling Miaomiao carefully examined the appearance of the person in the mirror: a pale white face, long slender eyebrows, attractively round eyes, thin lips, small and pointed chin – a beautiful girl of humble background.

If the distance between her lustrous pupils were just a bit smaller, Ling Yu could have become a foxy beauty with alluring eyes shining like spring water, capable of bringing cities to ruin like the breathtaking seductress Da Ji. But her pupils were spaced too widely apart, giving her a gentle and unassertive disposition. Even if she were to glare at someone, she’d still look very sweet and innocent.

Ling Miaomiao sighed deeply. Based on her appearance, she simply was not destined to become a heroine.

She stroked her pointed chin, slightly furrowing her eyebrows.

Ling Yu looked too scrawny, so skinny that it made other people uncomfortable. Throughout the ages, people believed that plump and supple-bodied women were blessed with great fortune. According to this ancient verbiage, her face was cursed with rotten luck and a short-lived existence.

Ling Miaomiao stood up, her bright red wedding dress flopping onto the floor. The marriage ceremony was hurriedly staged, and since the dress was haphazardly borrowed from somewhere, it didn’t fit at all. The loose waist was fastened by a thin silver needle, and the overly wide cuffs completely covered her dainty hands. The golden embroidery laid obscured amongst the clothing folds, making it impossible to notice the intricate details of the dress.

Ling Yu was as thin as a bean sprout. Her bad habit of hunching over and lowering her head resulted in a tendency to slump her shoulders forward, further kindling a rather cowardly impression.

Ling Miaomiao forcibly straightened her back and cast a side glance at the mirror. What peered back was an unfamiliar impatiently frowning face, frightening her to promptly smoothen out her knitted brows – perhaps it’s because Miaomiao already had a bad impression of Ling Yu prior to possessing her body, but she even detested her physical appearance. This seriously shouldn’t be happening!

In this era, it was very common for people to traverse between parallel worlds. Any random occurrence in life could become the catalyst for interdimensional travel.

The reason Ling Miaomiao entered the body of the young girl “Ling Yu”, standing in wedding apparel in the middle of the night, was because she had written a scathing book review out of righteous indignation.
